summary: Clean front‑of‑house recording of a high‑energy 1991 show with standout Help > Slipknot, Fire and an emotional Morning Dew.
review: The audience (FOB) tape is surprisingly clear for a rowdy venue, especially after EQ work, though a stray whistling during Birdsong mars a few seconds. Musically the concert shines – Garcia nails every lyric in Help on the Way and launches into a flawless Slipknot intro, the roof blows off when Fire opens, and Hornsby’s piano on Morning Dew moves listeners to tears.

ai_rating.rationale: Four detailed reviews praise the performance (standout songs, strong playing) and note a generally good recording with only minor noise issues.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Overall clean and well‑EQ'd front‑of‑house capture; occasional intrusive yeller/whistler during Birdsong but crowd ambience is well‑controlled.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy first set leading into a powerful second half; the band rides the crowd’s excitement, culminating in an explosive Fire and a heartfelt Morning Dew.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s vocals are spot‑on for Help on the Way and his guitar sparks the roof‑blowing Fire intro.
band_member_comments.Keys: Bruce Hornsby delivers powerful, emotive piano work, especially on Morning Dew.
